question: Design a C++ class named `Book` with private attributes for `title` (string), `author` (string), and `isbn` (string). Include public methods `setTitle`, `setAuthor`, `setISBN` to set these values, and `displayBookInfo` to print all book details. Create an object of `Book` and demonstrate its usage.

test_cases.expected:
Title: Effective Modern C++
Author: Scott Meyers
ISBN: 978-1491903995
--- Another Book ---
Title: Clean Code
Author: Robert C. Martin
ISBN: 978-0132350884

errors: The `displayBookInfo` method has a logical error: it always prints a fixed string for ISBN ('Fixed ISBN-12345') instead of the actual `isbn` attribute of the Book object.
